title: Matthew Henry's Complete Commentary on the Whole Bible
author: Matthew Henry
tradition_notes: Henry was a Welsh Nonconformist (Presbyterian) minister, strongly influenced by Puritan theology. His commentary is the most widely used devotional/practical commentary in the Reformed tradition. Published 1706-1714; Genesis through Acts completed by Henry, Romans through Revelation finished posthumously by colleagues from his notes.
notes: Henry died 1714 -- text is public domain in all jurisdictions. HelloAO marks this PDM 1.0. Coverage: 65 books (Song of Solomon missing from HelloAO data; Matthew only has chapters 1-18; Jonah only has chapter 1).
known_coverage_gaps: Song of Solomon (SNG) -- not available in HelloAO MH data
Matthew (MAT) -- HelloAO only has chapters 1-18 (not all 28)
Jonah (JON) -- HelloAO only has chapter 1 (not all 4)
